devanagari: अथ षट्षष्टितमोऽध्यायः । श्रीशुक उवाच । नन्दव्रजं गते रामे करूषाधिपतिर्नृप । वासुदेवोऽहमित्यज्ञो दूतं कृष्णाय प्राहिणोत् ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: atha ṣaṭṣaṣṭitamo śrīśuka uvāca nandavrajaṃ gate rāme karūṣādhipatirnṛpa vāsudevo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
When Balarāma had gone to the Vraja under Nanda’s control, the ruler of the country called Karuṣa[1] being full of ignorance, sent to Kṛṣṇa a messenger declaring ‘I am (the real) Vasudeva.’
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Kauṭalīya Arthaśāstra notes its fame for excellent elephants. D.C. Sircar however identifies Karuṣa with Shahabad region of South West Bihar (SGAMI-332-35). This explains Pauṇḍraka Vāsudeva’s friendship with the King of Vārāṇasī and his stay at Vāraṇasī whence he advanced against Kṛṣṇa.

devanagari: त्वं वासुदेवो भगवानवतीर्णो जगत्पतिः । इति प्रस्तोभितो बालैर्मेन आत्मानमच्युतम् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: tvaṃ vāsudevo bhagavānavatīrṇo jagatpatiḥ iti prastobhito bālairmena ātmānamacyutam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: “You are the glorious Lord Vāsudeva, the ruler and the protector of the universe who have incarnated (for the protection of the world)”. Thus flattered by foolish people, he really came to believe that he was the Imperishable Lord.

devanagari: दूतं च प्राहिणोन्मन्दः कृष्णायाव्यक्तवर्त्मने । द्वारकायां यथा बालो नृपो बालकृतोऽबुधः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: dūtaṃ ca prāhiṇonmandaḥ kṛṣṇāyāvyaktavartmane dvārakāyāṃ yathā bālo nṛpo bālakṛto

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

translation: And the dull-witted fellow (being ignorant of the real greatness of Kṛṣṇa) sent an emissary to Lord Kṛṣṇa of inscrutable ways, at Dvārakā, even as an ignorant boy who is set up as a king by other (equally ignorant) children in children’s play would do.

devanagari: दूतस्तु द्वारकामेत्य सभायामास्थितं प्रभुम् । कृष्णं कमलपत्राक्षं राजसन्देशमब्रवीत् ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: dūtastu dvārakāmetya sabhāyāmāsthitaṃ prabhum kṛṣṇaṃ kamalapatrākṣaṃ rājasandeśamabravīt
translation: The messenger on his part arrived at Dvārakā and conveyed the following message of his king to the Almighty lotus-eyed Kṛṣṇa as he was attending the court-assembly.

devanagari: वासुदेवोऽवतीर्णोहमेक एव न चापरः । भूतानामनुकम्पार्थं त्वं तु मिथ्याभिधां त्यज ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: vāsudevo bhūtānāmanukampārthaṃ tvaṃ tu mithyābhidhāṃ tyaja
translation: “I am the only one Vāsudeva who have descended (as an incarnation on the earth) for showing compassion to all created beings. There is no other Vāsudeva. Hence you relinquish your false title.

devanagari: यानि त्वमस्मच्चिह्नानि मौढ्याद्बिभर्षि सात्वत । त्यक्त्वैहि मां त्वं शरणं नो चेद्देहि ममाहवम् ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: yāni tvamasmaccihnāni mauḍhyādbibharṣi sātvata tyaktvaihi māṃ tvaṃ śaraṇaṃ no ceddehi mamāhavam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: O Sātvata! Whatever emblems of ours (such as Kaustubha Vanamālā, conch, a discus etc.) you bear out of your stupidity, renounce them and come to me for shelter. If not, give a fight with me.”

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । कत्थनं तदुपाकर्ण्य पौण्ड्रकस्याल्पमेधसः । उग्रसेनादयः सभ्या उच्चकैर्जहसुस्तदा ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca katthanaṃ tadupākarṇya pauṇḍrakasyālpamedhasaḥ ugrasenādayaḥ sabhyā uccakairjahasustadā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Hearing that boasting of that puny-minded king Pauṇḍraka Ugrasena and other members of the assembly laughed loudly.

devanagari: उवाच दूतं भगवान्परिहासकथामनु । उत्स्रक्ष्ये मूढ चिह्नानि यैस्त्वमेवं विकत्थसे ॥ ८ ॥
translation: After jocular pleasantries, the glorious Lord replied (through the same) messenger. “O fool, I shall hurl my insignia (such as discus, mace etc.) at you, your emblems and supporters on the strength of which you brag thus (Or: I shall relieve you of all your artificial emblems like Sudarśana etc. on the strength of which you are boasting).

devanagari: मुखं तदपिधायाज्ञ कङ्कगृध्रवटैर्वृतः । शयिष्यसे हतस्तत्र भविता शरणं शुनाम् ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: mukhaṃ tadapidhāyājña kaṅkagṛdhravaṭairvṛtaḥ śayiṣyase hatastatra bhavitā śaraṇaṃ śunām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ vṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 9 verb]
[nominative single from √ vṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 9 verb]
[nominative single from √ han class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ han class 2 verb]

translation: (As to your demand for my submission to you) It is you who will be the resort (prey) of dogs with that (braggart) mouth of yours shut. You will lie down dead on the battlefield surrounded by kites, vultures and other birds of prey, you fool.”

devanagari: इति दूतस्तमाक्षेपं स्वामिने सर्वमाहरत् । कृष्णोऽपि रथमास्थाय काशीमुपजगाम ह ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: iti dūtastamākṣepaṃ svāmine sarvamāharat kṛṣṇo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active first plural], [injunctive active first plural]

translation: The messenger conveyed that defiant censure to his master in full details. Śrī Kṛṣṇa also mounted his chariot, went to Kāśī (where Pauṇḍraka was staying with his friend, the king of Kāśī).

devanagari: पौण्ड्रकोऽपि तदुद्योगमुपलभ्य महारथः । अक्षौहिणीभ्यां संयुक्तो निश्चक्राम पुराद्द्रुतम् ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: pauṇḍrako akṣauhiṇībhyāṃ saṃyukto niścakrāma purāddrutam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Hearing the intelligence of Kṛṣṇa’s invasion, even the mighty car-warrior Pauṇḍraka quickly sallied forth from the town (Kāśī) with two akṣauhiṇīs of army under his command.

devanagari: तस्य काशीपतिर्मित्रं पार्ष्णिग्राहोऽन्वयान्नृप । अक्षौहिणीभिस्तिसृभिरपश्यत्पौण्ड्रकं हइः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: tasya kāśīpatirmitraṃ pārṣṇigrāho akṣauhiṇībhistisṛbhirapaśyatpauṇḍrakaṃ haiḥ
translation: His friend, the king of Kāśī followed him to guard his rear with his army of three akṣauhiṇīs, O king. Hari espied Pauṇḍraka:

devanagari: शङ्खार्यसिगदाशार्ङ्ग श्रीवत्साद्युपलक्षितम् । बिभ्राणं कौस्तुभमणिं वनमालाविभूषितम् ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: śaṅkhāryasigadāśārṅga śrīvatsādyupalakṣitam bibhrāṇaṃ kaustubhamaṇiṃ vanamālāvibhūṣitam
translation: Who (Pauṇḍraka) was bearing the (replicas of Kṛṣṇa’s arms such as) the (Pāñcajanya) conch, the (Nandaka) sword, the (Kaumodakī) mace, the Śārṅga bow; was distinguished by the Śrīvatsa mark (on the chest), was wearing (an artificial) Kaustubha gem and was decorated with Vanamālā. He was clad in two pieces of yellow silk and had an ensign of Garuḍa (on his chariot); he was adorned with a priceless crown and very costly ornaments and was wearing two radiant ear-rings of alligator-shape.

devanagari: कौशेयवाससी पीते वसानं गरुडध्वजम् । अमूल्यमौल्याभरणं स्फुरन्मकरकुण्डलम् ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: kauśeyavāsasī pīte vasānaṃ garuḍadhvajam amūlyamaulyābharaṇaṃ sphuranmakarakuṇḍalam

translation: Who (Pauṇḍraka) was bearing the (replicas of Kṛṣṇa’s arms such as) the (Pāñcajanya) conch, the (Nandaka) sword, the (Kaumodakī) mace, the Śārṅga bow; was distinguished by the Śrīvatsa mark (on the chest), was wearing (an artificial) Kaustubha gem and was decorated with Vanamālā. He was clad in two pieces of yellow silk and had an ensign of Garuḍa (on his chariot); he was adorned with a priceless crown and very costly ornaments and was wearing two radiant ear-rings of alligator-shape.

devanagari: दृष्ट्वा तमात्मनस्तुल्यं वेषं कृत्रिममास्थितम् । यथा नटं रङ्गगतं विजहास भृशं हरीः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: dṛṣṭvā tamātmanastulyaṃ veṣaṃ kṛtrimamāsthitam yathā naṭaṃ raṅgagataṃ vijahāsa bhṛśaṃ harīḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ tul class 10 verb], [accusative single from √ tul class 10 verb]

translation:
Seeing him seated (in the chariot) with an artificial dress similar to His own like an actor on the stage, Lord Hari burst into a heavy laugh.
[Padaratnāvalī’s Text adds:]
15(A). The battalion of archers of the king of Kāśī, equipped with strong bows, covered Kṛṣṇa with arrows within a moment, just as rain-clouds cover a mountain with water. Some of them were seated on elephants, some on horses and some mounted in chariots, all of them marched forward to the battlefront.

devanagari: शूलैर्गदाभिः परिघैः शक्त्यृष्टिप्रासतोमरैः । असिभिः पट्टिशैर्बाणैः प्राहरन्नरयो हरिम् ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: śūlairgadābhiḥ parighaiḥ śaktyṛṣṭiprāsatomaraiḥ asibhiḥ paṭṭiśairbāṇaiḥ prāharannarayo harim
translation:
The enemies (mercilessly) attacked Hari with darts, maces, iron bars, javelins, ṛṣṭis (double-edged swords) different kinds of spears e.g. Tomara, swords and arrows.
[Padaratnāvalī’s Text adds:]
16(A). Another king, a friend of the ruler of Kāśī came to his help with three akṣauhiṇīs of army, in that battle.

devanagari: कृष्णस्तु तत्पौण्ड्रककाशिराजयोर् । बलं गजस्यन्दनवाजिपत्तिमत् । गदासिचक्रेषुभिरार्दयद्भृशं । यथा युगान्ते हुतभुक्पृथक्प्रजाः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: kṛṣṇastu tatpauṇḍrakakāśirājayor balaṃ gajasyandanavājipattimat gadāsicakreṣubhirārdayadbhṛśaṃ yathā yugānte hutabhukpṛthakprajāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Just as the cosmic fire bursting forth at the end of yugas completely consumes all the categories of creatures, Śrī Kṛṣṇa with his mace, sword, discus and arrows, totally annihilated the armies of Pauṇḍraka and Kāśirāja consisting of elephant-divisions, cavalry, chariots and foot-soldiers.

devanagari: आयोधनं तद्र थवाजिकुञ्जर द्विपत्खरोष्ट्रैररिणावखण्डितैः । बभौ चितं मोदवहं मनस्विनामाक्रीडनं भूतपतेरिवोल्बणम् ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: āyodhanaṃ tadra thavājikuñjara dvipatkharoṣṭrairariṇāvakhaṇḍitaiḥ babhau citaṃ modavahaṃ manasvināmākrīḍanaṃ bhūtapaterivolbaṇam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active second single], [imperfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: That battlefield was scattered over with (parts and limbs of) chariots, horses, elephants, foot-soldiers, donkeys and camels which were cut down and amputated by his discus. Like the terrible dancing arena of Rudra, the battlefield shone, heartening only the brave.

devanagari: अथाह पौण्ड्रकं शौरिर्भो भो पौण्ड्रक यद्भवान् । दूतवाक्येन मामाह तान्यस्त्राण्युत्सृजामि ते ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: athāha pauṇḍrakaṃ śaurirbho bho pauṇḍraka yadbhavān dūtavākyena māmāha tānyastrāṇyutsṛjāmi te

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Kṛṣṇa, the descendant of Śūra, (sarcastically) said to Pauṇḍraka, “O Pauṇḍraka, whatever weapons you asked me to surrender through your messenger, I now discharge at you.

devanagari: त्याजयिष्येऽभिधानं मे यत्त्वयाज्ञ मृषा धृतम् । व्रजामि शरणं तेऽद्य यदि नेच्छामि संयुगम् ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: tyājayiṣye vrajāmi śaraṇaṃ te

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: I shall make you give up my epithet which you have falsely assumed, you ignorant fellow. And if I were not to desire to fight with you, I would seek you as a Protector today”.

devanagari: इति क्षिप्त्वा शितैर्बाणैर्विरथीकृत्य पौण्ड्रकम् । शिरोऽवृश्चद्रथाङ्गेन वज्रेणेन्द्रो यथा गिरेः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: iti kṣiptvā śitairbāṇairvirathīkṛtya pauṇḍrakam śiro

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [instrumental plural from √ śā class 3 verb], [instrumental plural from √ śā class 4 verb]
[instrumental plural from √ śā class 3 verb], [instrumental plural from √ śā class 4 verb]
[vocative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [vocative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]
[vocative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [vocative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

translation: With this sarcastic reply (with reference to Pauṇḍraka’s message with his emissary), he deprived Pauṇḍraka of his chariots with his sharp shafts. And just as Indra lopped of the tops (or wings) of mountain, he sundered off the head of Pauṇḍraka with his discus.

devanagari: तथा काशीपतेः कायाच्छिर उत्कृत्य पत्रिभिः । न्यपातयत्काशीपुर्यां पद्मकोशमिवानिलः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: tathā kāśīpateḥ kāyācchira utkṛtya patribhiḥ nyapātayatkāśīpuryāṃ padmakośamivānilaḥ
translation: Similarly, he chopped off with his arrows the head of Kāśirāja from his body and made it fall down in the city of Kāśī like a lotus bud carried with it by wind.

devanagari: एवं मत्सरिणम्हत्वा पौण्ड्रकं ससखं हरिः । द्वारकामाविशत्सिद्धैर्गीयमानकथामृतः ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ matsariṇamhatvā pauṇḍrakaṃ sasakhaṃ hariḥ dvārakāmāviśatsiddhairgīyamānakathāmṛtaḥ
translation: Having thus killed the jealous Pauṇḍraka along with his friend Kāśīrāja, he entered the city of Dvārakā while his nectarlike sweet exploits were being glorified in songs by Siddhas.

devanagari: स नित्यं भगवद्ध्यान प्रध्वस्ताखिलबन्धनः । बिभ्राणश्च हरे राजन्स्वरूपं तन्मयोऽभवत् ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: sa nityaṃ bhagavaddhyāna pradhvastākhilabandhanaḥ bibhrāṇaśca hare rājansvarūpaṃ tanmayo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

translation: Due to the continuous contemplation of Hari, all the (Karma) bondages of Pauṇḍraka were completely annihilated. As he always put on a form similar to that of Hari, he became one with Hari.

devanagari: शिरः पतितमालोक्य राजद्वारे सकुण्डलम् । किमिदं कस्य वा वक्त्रमिति संशिशिरे जनाः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: śiraḥ patitamālokya rājadvāre sakuṇḍalam kimidaṃ kasya vā vaktramiti saṃśiśire janāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ pat class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ pat class 1 verb]

translation: Seeing the head adorned with ear-rings fallen at the palace-gate, people were puzzled as to what it was and whose head it was.

devanagari: राज्ञः काशीपतेर्ज्ञात्वा महिष्यः पुत्रबान्धवाः । पौराश्च हा हता राजन्नाथ नाथेति प्रारुदन् ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: rājñaḥ kāśīpaterjñātvā mahiṣyaḥ putrabāndhavāḥ paurāśca hā hatā rājannātha nātheti prārudan

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ han class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ han class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ han class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ han class 2 verb]

[nominative plural from √ han class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ han class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ han class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ han class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ han class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ han class 2 verb]
[present middle first single], [imperative active second single]

translation: When they came to know that it was the head of their own king, queens, princes, relatives and citizens of Kāśī lamented loudly “Alas. We are undone. O king, O dear Lord, O Lord”.

devanagari: सुदक्षिणस्तस्य सुतः कृत्वा संस्थाविधिं पतेः । निहत्य पितृहन्तारं यास्याम्यपचितिं पितुः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: sudakṣiṇastasya sutaḥ kṛtvā saṃsthāvidhiṃ pateḥ nihatya pitṛhantāraṃ yāsyāmyapacitiṃ pituḥ
translation: His crown Prince Sudakṣiṇa performed the funeral rites of his father. He determined, “I shall repay the debt of my father by killing his slayer”. Sudakṣiṇa along with his family priest propitiated god Śiva with the deepest meditation.

devanagari: इत्यात्मनाभिसन्धाय सोपाध्यायो महेश्वरम् । सुदक्षिणोऽर्चयामास परमेण समाधिना ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: ityātmanābhisandhāya sopādhyāyo maheśvaram sudakṣiṇo
translation: His crown Prince Sudakṣiṇa performed the funeral rites of his father. He determined, “I shall repay the debt of my father by killing his slayer”. Sudakṣiṇa along with his family priest propitiated god Śiva with the deepest meditation.

devanagari: प्रीतोऽविमुक्ते भगवांस्तस्मै वरमदाद्विभुः । पितृहन्तृवधोपायं स वव्रे वरमीप्सितम् ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: prīto pitṛhantṛvadhopāyaṃ sa vavre varamīpsitam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ prī class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ prī class 9 verb]
[imperfect active third single], [aorist active third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[nominative single from √ āp ], [accusative single from √ āp ]

translation: In the holy place of Avimuktaka (or Kāśī), god Śiva who was pleased with him, offered him the booh (of his choice). He sought his desired-most boon, viz. the means to slay his father’s slayer.

devanagari: दक्षिणाग्निं परिचर ब्राह्मणैः सममृत्विजम् । अभिचारविधानेन स चाग्निः प्रमथैर्वृतः ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: dakṣiṇāgniṃ paricara brāhmaṇaiḥ samamṛtvijam abhicāravidhānena sa cāgniḥ pramathairvṛtaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ vṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 9 verb]
[nominative single from √ vṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 9 verb]

translation: (Lord Śiva advised): ‘You should worship the fire known as Dakṣiṇāgni[2] (which is also called ṛtvij)[3] with the help of Brāhmaṇas according to the procedure prescribed for mastering black magic. That fire surrounded by Pramathas (goblins) will accomplish your purpose like a family priest (ṛtvij) appointed for a purpose, provided it is used against a person who is hostile to Brāhmaṇas and is hated by them. (This implies a warning not to use it against a friend of Brāhmaṇas like Kṛṣṇa). Observing strictly the prescribed vows, Sudakṣiṇa obeyed the directions of Śiva (for invoking the malevolent aspect of Dakṣiṇāgni) and directed it against Kṛṣṇa.
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Out of the three fires maintained in the family, Dakṣiṇāgni is to be propitiated for the worship of manes and mastery over black magic (abhicāra).

Footnote [3]:
ṛtvij is an epithet of Agni, vide Ṛg Veda Saṃhitā 1.1.1 This is one interpretation.

devanagari: साधयिष्यति सङ्कल्पमब्रह्मण्ये प्रयोजितः । इत्यादिष्टस्तथा चक्रेकृष्णायाभिचरन्व्रती ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: sādhayiṣyati saṅkalpamabrahmaṇye prayojitaḥ ityādiṣṭastathā cakrekṛṣṇāyābhicaranvratī

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ ji class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ ji class 9 verb]

translation: (Lord Śiva advised): ‘You should worship the fire known as Dakṣiṇāgni[2] (which is also called ṛtvij)[3] with the help of Brāhmaṇas according to the procedure prescribed for mastering black magic. That fire surrounded by Pramathas (goblins) will accomplish your purpose like a family priest (ṛtvij) appointed for a purpose, provided it is used against a person who is hostile to Brāhmaṇas and is hated by them. (This implies a warning not to use it against a friend of Brāhmaṇas like Kṛṣṇa). Observing strictly the prescribed vows, Sudakṣiṇa obeyed the directions of Śiva (for invoking the malevolent aspect of Dakṣiṇāgni) and directed it against Kṛṣṇa.
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Out of the three fires maintained in the family, Dakṣiṇāgni is to be propitiated for the worship of manes and mastery over black magic (abhicāra).

Footnote [3]:
ṛtvij is an epithet of Agni, vide Ṛg Veda Saṃhitā 1.1.1 This is one interpretation.

devanagari: ततोऽग्निरुत्थितः कुण्डान्मूर्तिमानतिभीषणः । तप्तताम्रशिखाश्मश्रुरङ्गारोद्गारिलोचनः ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: tato taptatāmraśikhāśmaśruraṅgārodgārilocanaḥ
translation: As soon as the abhicāra-prayoga (sacrifice for mastery over black magic) was completed, there arose from the sacrificial pit, god Agni in person, but in his very terrific form. The locks of his hair, beard and moustaches were of red-hot copper. He was emitting flames of fire from his eyes.

devanagari: दंष्ट्रोग्रभ्रुकुटीदण्ड कठोरास्यः स्वजिह्वया । आलिहन्सृक्वणी नग्नो विधुन्वंस्त्रिशिखं ज्वलत् ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: daṃṣṭrograbhrukuṭīdaṇḍa kaṭhorāsyaḥ svajihvayā ālihansṛkvaṇī nagno vidhunvaṃstriśikhaṃ jvalat

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ jval class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ jval class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ jval class 1 verb]

translation: He had a very grim appearance with large protruding tusks and staff-like long eyebrows. He was licking the ends of his mouth. He was naked and was brandishing a trident that threw out flames of fire.

devanagari: पद्भ्यां तालप्रमाणाभ्यां कम्पयन्नवनीतलम् । सोऽयधावद्वृतो भूतैर्द्वारकां प्रदहन्दिशः ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: padbhyāṃ tālapramāṇābhyāṃ kampayannavanītalam so

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ kamp ], [vocative single from √ kamp ]
[nominative single from √ vṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 9 verb]
[nominative single from √ vṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 9 verb]

translation: Surrounded by goblins and setting ablaze all the cardinal points, he rushed against Dvārakā shaking the earth under his strides, with his legs tall as a palm tree.

devanagari: तमाभिचारदहनमायान्तं द्वारकौकसः । विलोक्य तत्रसुः सर्वे वनदाहे मृगा यथा ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: tamābhicāradahanamāyāntaṃ dvārakaukasaḥ vilokya tatrasuḥ sarve vanadāhe mṛgā yathā
translation: Seeing that Abhicāra (malevolent) fire rushing towards them, all the citizens of Dvārakā were terribly afraid like wild beasts at the approach of the forest conflagration.

devanagari: अक्षैः सभायां क्रीडन्तं भगवन्तं भयातुराः । त्राहि त्राहि त्रिलोकेश वह्नेः प्रदहतः पुरम् ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: akṣaiḥ sabhāyāṃ krīḍantaṃ bhagavantaṃ bhayāturāḥ trāhi trāhi trilokeśa vahneḥ pradahataḥ puram

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ han class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ han class 2 verb]

translation: While the glorious Lord was playing dice in the assembly-hall, they ran to him overwhelmed with fear and prayed, “Protect us, O Lord of the three worlds, protect us from the fire that is burning the city”.

devanagari: श्रुत्वा तज्जनवैक्लव्यं दृष्ट्वा स्वानां च साध्वसम् । शरण्यः सम्प्रहस्याह मा भैष्टेत्यवितास्म्यहम् ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: śrutvā tajjanavaiklavyaṃ dṛṣṭvā svānāṃ ca sādhvasam śaraṇyaḥ samprahasyāha mā bhaiṣṭetyavitāsmyaham

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ klu class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ klu class 1 verb]

translation: Listening to the (report of that) public calamity and seeing the cause of their panic, the Lord, who is the shelter of his devotees, laughed loudly and assured them: Don’t be afraid. I shall be your saviour”.

devanagari: सर्वस्यान्तर्बहिःसाक्षी कृत्यां माहेश्वरीं विभुः । विज्ञाय तद्विघातार्थं पार्श्वस्थं चक्रमादिशत् ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: sarvasyāntarbahiḥsākṣī kṛtyāṃ māheśvarīṃ vibhuḥ vijñāya tadvighātārthaṃ pārśvasthaṃ cakramādiśat

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural]

translation: The Almighty Lord who is a witness to whatever happens within and without all created beings, came to know it to be the Kṛīyā (female form of the malevolent force) of god Śiva and commanded his discus Sudarśana that was by his side, to destroy her.

devanagari: तत्सूर्यकोटिप्रतिमं सुदर्शनं जाज्वल्यमानं प्रलयानलप्रभम् । स्वतेजसा खं ककुभोऽथ रोदसी चक्रं मुकुन्दास्त्रं अथाग्निमार्दयत् ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: tatsūryakoṭipratimaṃ sudarśanaṃ jājvalyamānaṃ pralayānalaprabham svatejasā khaṃ kakubho

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: The Sudarśana discus, the missile discharged by Lord Kṛṣṇa was dazzling like millions of Suns (combined). It was flaming forth like the cosmic fire destroying the universe (at the end of yugas). It was radiating its brilliant lustre in all directions, the heaven and the earth. It forthwith extinguished that Abhicāra fire.

devanagari: कृत्यानलः प्रतिहतः स रथान्गपाणेर् । अस्त्रौजसा स नृप भग्नमुखो निवृत्तः । वाराणसीं परिसमेत्य सुदक्षिणं तं । सर्त्विग्जनं समदहत्स्वकृतोऽ...
english_devnagari: kṛtyānalaḥ pratihataḥ sa rathāngapāṇer astraujasā sa nṛpa bhagnamukho nivṛttaḥ vārāṇasīṃ parisametya sudakṣiṇaṃ taṃ sartvigjanaṃ samadahatsvakṛto

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb]
[nominative single from √ sam class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ sam class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ sam class 1 verb]

translation: Counteracted by the force of the missile (Sudarśana) of Lord Kṛṣṇa, the Abhicāra fire called Kṛtyā had all its force curbed down and blunted. As if slapped in the face, it turned back, approached Vārāṇasī and burnt down Sudakṣiṇa and his family-priests who directed the Kṛtyā.

devanagari: चक्रं च विष्णोस्तदनुप्रविष्टं वाराणसीं साट्टसभालयापणाम् । सगोपुराट्टालककोष्ठसङ्कुलां सकोशहस्त्यश्वरथान्नशालिनीम् ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: cakraṃ ca viṣṇostadanupraviṣṭaṃ vārāṇasīṃ sāṭṭasabhālayāpaṇām sagopurāṭṭālakakoṣṭhasaṅkulāṃ sakośahastyaśvarathānnaśālinīm
translation: Close on its heels, the discus of Viṣṇu entered the city of Vārāṇasī and reduced it to ashes along with its high towers, assembly halls, mansions, market places, towers, enclosures, treasuries, arches, stalls for elephants, horses, chariots, dining halls and kitchens and returned to Dvārakā again and stood by the side of Kṛṣṇa who does everything sportively.

devanagari: दग्ध्वा वाराणसीं सर्वां विष्णोश्चक्रं सुदर्शनम् । भूयः पार्श्वमुपातिष्ठत्कृष्णस्याक्लिष्टकर्मणः ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: dagdhvā vārāṇasīṃ sarvāṃ viṣṇoścakraṃ sudarśanam bhūyaḥ pārśvamupātiṣṭhatkṛṣṇasyākliṣṭakarmaṇaḥ
translation: Close on its heels, the discus of Viṣṇu entered the city of Vārāṇasī and reduced it to ashes along with its high towers, assembly halls, mansions, market places, towers, enclosures, treasuries, arches, stalls for elephants, horses, chariots, dining halls and kitchens and returned to Dvārakā again and stood by the side of Kṛṣṇa who does everything sportively.

devanagari: य एनं श्रावयेन्मर्त्य उत्तमःश्लोकविक्रमम् । समाहितो वा शृणुयात्सर्वपापैः प्रमुच्यते ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: ya enaṃ śrāvayenmartya uttamaḥślokavikramam samāhito vā śṛṇuyātsarvapāpaiḥ pramucyate
translation:
The mortal being who listens to this attentively or relates this exploit of the most glorious Lord to others becomes completely absolved of all sins.
[Padaratnāvalī’s Text adds:]
43(A). Both of them (the narrator and listener of exploits) rid themselves of all sins including heinous ones like Brahmahatyā and when they cast off their body, they proceed to the heaven of Lord Viṣṇu.
